fields.description:
## Why

The 2026-06-18 audit found five genuine duplication clusters (validated by jscpd +
manual reading + the plan-review pass): repeated logic where a bug must be fixed in
two places. Two are correctness-/concurrency-critical (the advisory-lock CAS twins
and the blocker-edge inversion). DRYing them removes a class of
fix-one-forget-the-other bugs.

## What

Extract shared helpers for the five confirmed clusters: F9 (LLM finalize bypass),
F7 (ticket-schema dict), F5 (blocker inversion), F2/F3 (resolve-id-or-emit), F4
(advisory-lock CAS frame).

## Scope

- `llm/code_review.py`, `llm/spec_scan.py`, `llm/aggregate.py` (F9).
- `reducer/_state.py` (F7).
- `graph/_relations.py`, `graph/_ready.py`, `graph/_unblock.py` (F5).
- `_commands/transition.py` (F2/F3).
- `_engine/rebar_reconciler/_advisory_lock.py` (F4).
- Out of scope: the parallel-by-design interface signatures; the genuinely-inverting reconciler differs.

## Delivery notes (deviations from the plan, recorded honestly)

- **F5 home:** the shared `build_blocked_by` went into the dependency-free
  `_relations.py`, NOT `_blockers.py` ŌĆö `_blockers.py` imports the heavy loader and
  `_ready` is deliberately kept off it. (Bonus: removed `_unblock.py`'s third copy of
  the `_BLOCKING_RELATIONS` constant.)
- **F2/F3 "compact drift" was a misread:** `compact_cli` is text-only (no
  `--output`/json mode), so there is no `ticket_not_found` envelope to emit ŌĆö left
  unchanged. The real duplication was the identical resolve-id-or-emit block in
  `transition_cli` ŌēĪ `claim_cli`, deduped via a module-local `_resolve_id_or_report`
  (the block is CLI-output-aware ŌĆö `error_envelope`/stdout/stderr ŌĆö so it belongs in
  `transition.py`, not the write-composition `_seam`).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] `code_review.py` and `spec_scan.py` call `finalize_findings`; `_SEVERITY_RANK`
  is defined once (`aggregate._severity_rank`).
- [x] `make_error_dict` is built from `make_initial_state`; both carry
  `preconditions_summary` and the same base key set (drift fixed).
- [x] The `blocked_by` inversion exists once (`_relations.build_blocked_by`) and both
  `_ready.py`/`_unblock.py` use it; the duplicate relations constant is gone.
- [x] `transition_cli`/`claim_cli` share `_resolve_id_or_report` (compact left as-is ŌĆö
  text-only, no envelope; documented above).
- [x] The advisory-lock CAS frame is extracted once
  (`_commit_in_detached_tickets_worktree`); the delete predicate is normalized to the
  staged-diff guard; the advisory-lock + reconciler suites pass (312 tests).
- [x] ruff (on changed files) + the affected suites stay green; no behavior change
  except the F7 `preconditions_summary` fix.
</content>
